report_name: SideCopy APT Targets Afghanistan's Finance Ministry in 'XENOFISCAL' Espionage Campaign

threat_actors.name: SideCopy
threat_actors.description: A Pakistan-aligned APT group, considered a subgroup of Transparent Tribe (APT36), conducting the 'Operation XENOFISCAL' cyber-espionage campaign against Afghanistan's Ministry of Finance. The group uses spear-phishing with Pashto-language lures to deploy XenoRAT.
threat_actors.goals: Cyber-espionage, data theft, and surveillance against government and military targets in South Asia.

threat_actors.name: Transparent Tribe
threat_actors.description: A Pakistan-aligned threat group, also known as APT36, that acts as an umbrella group for other actors, including SideCopy. This campaign is attributed to its SideCopy subgroup.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Attachment
attack_patterns.description: The attack begins with a spear-phishing email containing a ZIP archive with a malicious LNK file, sent to officials at Afghanistan's Ministry of Finance.

attack_patterns.name: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: Victims are tricked into executing a malicious LNK file disguised with a Pashto filename, which initiates the infection chain.

attack_patterns.name: System Binary Proxy Execution: Mshta
attack_patterns.description: The malicious LNK file executes mshta.exe to download and run a remote HTML Application (HTA) file from a compromised domain.

attack_patterns.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
attack_patterns.description: XenoRAT establishes persistence by creating a registry run key, often mimicking a legitimate application like Microsoft Edge, to ensure it executes on system startup.

attack_patterns.name: Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: The infection chain uses mshta.exe to download a remote HTA file over HTTP. The final XenoRAT payload communicates with its C2 server over TCP, which may use web protocols.

attack_patterns.name: Obtain Capabilities: Tool
attack_patterns.description: SideCopy obtained the open-source XenoRAT for use as the final payload in their espionage campaign.

malware.name: XenoRAT
malware.description: An open-source remote access trojan (version 1.8.7 mentioned) deployed as the final payload. It provides attackers with full remote access for keylogging, screen capture, file system access, and data exfiltration.

tools.name: mshta.exe
tools.description: The legitimate Windows utility mshta.exe was abused by the attackers to execute a remote HTML Application (HTA) file from a compromised server as part of the infection chain.

campaigns.name: Operation XENOFISCAL
campaigns.description: A targeted cyber-espionage campaign by the SideCopy APT group against Afghanistan's Ministry of Finance, using spear-phishing to deploy the XenoRAT trojan.
campaigns.objective: Espionage and data exfiltration from the Afghan Ministry of Finance.

identities.name: Afghanistan Ministry of Finance
identities.description: The primary target of the 'Operation XENOFISCAL' campaign. The attack specifically focused on its 34 provincial revenue and finance directorates (Mustoufiats) and Pashto-speaking officials.

infrastructure.name: Malicious HTA Hosting Server
infrastructure.description: A compromised Afghan educational domain (abimj.edu.af) used to host the malicious HTA file for the initial stage of the infection.

infrastructure.name: XenoRAT C2 Server
infrastructure.description: Command and control server (185.235.137.106) used by the XenoRAT payload to receive commands and exfiltrate data.

indicators.name: SideCopy XENOFISCAL C2 and Staging Infrastructure
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: SideCopy XENOFISCAL C2 and Staging Network Activity
id: 4f5e6d7c-8b9a-4123-b456-7890abcdef12
status: experimental
description: Detects network connections to domains and IPs associated with the SideCopy 'Operation XENOFISCAL' campaign.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/pakistan-linked-sidecopy-targets.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-02
tags:
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.g1008
level: high
logsource:
  category: network_traffic
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_domain:
    destination.domain: 'abimj.edu.af'
  selection_ip:
    destination.ip: '185.235.137.106'
  condition: 1 of them
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to the compromised HTA staging domain or the XenoRAT C2 server IP address identified in the Operation XENOFISCAL campaign.

indicators.name: Suspicious Remote HTA Execution via Mshta
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Remote HTA Execution via Mshta.exe
id: 9a8b7c6d-5e4f-4321-a0b9-c8d7e6f5a4b3
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of mshta.exe to execute a remote HTML Application (HTA) file, a technique used by SideCopy in the XENOFISCAL campaign.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/pakistan-linked-sidecopy-targets.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-02
tags:
  - attack.t1218.005
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\mshta.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'http://'
      - 'https://'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of mshta.exe with a command line argument containing a URL, which is indicative of remote HTA file execution used in this campaign.

indicators.name: Persistence via Registry Run Key
indicators.pattern:
title: Persistence via HKCU Run Key Creation
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-4789-890a-bcdef0123456
status: experimental
description: 'Detects the creation of a new value in the HKCU\...\Run registry key, a common persistence technique used by malware like XenoRAT.'
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/pakistan-linked-sidecopy-targets.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-02
tags:
  - attack.t1547.001
level: medium
logsource:
  category: registry_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    EventType: 'SetValue'
    TargetObject|contains: '\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of a new persistence entry in the HKEY_CURRENT_USER Run key, a method used by XenoRAT in this campaign.

relationships.target_name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
